brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +36.1% since Feb 27 — Brent $96.66 (Apr 10 close, last trading session) — Islamabad talks COLLAPSED after 21 hours — Trump announces US naval BLOCKADE of Hormuz — volatile Monday reopening expected
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 10 close: $96.66/bbl (Friday — markets closed Apr 11 Sat / Apr 12 Sun). Brent gave back war premium on last week's ceasefire optimism (-10-12% weekly) but remains elevated — physical dated Brent at $131.97/bbl on Apr 10 reflected $35 premium over futures from extreme physical scarcity. Islamabad talks collapsed early Sunday Apr 12 after 21 hours with no agreement; Trump immediately announced US Navy will 'begin the process of BLOCKADING any and all Ships trying to enter, or leave, the Strait of Hormuz' and interdict any vessel that paid Iran's toll. 600+ vessels (325 tankers) stranded (Lloyd's List). 13M bpd shut in (Energy Aspects). Markets reopen Monday under heavy reversal pressure — last week's gains likely to unwind. Islamabad failure + counter-blockade represents most significant escalation since Apr 8 ceasefire. Source: ICE Futures Europe / Bloomberg / ADNOC / Lloyd's List / Energy Aspects / Trump Truth Social.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +43.8% since Feb 27 — WTI $95.63 (Apr 10 close, last trading session) — Islamabad talks COLLAPSED — Trump announces Hormuz BLOCKADE — gas at $4.135 likely to spike Monday
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 10 close: $95.63/bbl (Friday — markets closed Apr 11 Sat / Apr 12 Sun). Down 12% for the week on ceasefire optimism, but Islamabad talks collapsed early Sunday Apr 12 after 21 hours and Trump declared a US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. AAA national average gas $4.135/gal (Apr 11). March CPI showed gasoline prices surging 21.2% in a single month — the largest monthly spike since 1967. Diesel at $5.670. California at $5.89, Oklahoma at $3.27. EIA forecasts April gasoline average peaking ~$4.30. Trump ordered Navy to interdict every vessel that paid Iran's toll, destroy Iranian mines, threatened to 'blow to hell' any Iranian who fires at US ships. Monday reopening expected to erase much of last week's decline. Source: NYMEX / AAA / BLS / Bloomberg / Trump Truth Social.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.155 / +38.8% since Feb 27 — $4.135 nationally (AAA Apr 11) — Hormuz BLOCKADE announcement likely to push prices higher — EIA forecasts April peak ~$4.30 — California $5.89
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). Current: $4.135/gal nationally (AAA Apr 11) — down marginally from $4.153 the prior day. Diesel at $5.670. Most expensive state: California at $5.89; cheapest: Oklahoma at $3.27. Gas crossed $4.00/gallon Apr 2 for first time since Aug 2022. EIA forecasts monthly average peaking ~$4.30 in April. March CPI (released Apr 10) confirmed gasoline prices surged 21.2% in a single month — the largest monthly spike since 1967. Energy overall +10.9% MoM, +12.5% YoY. Trump's Apr 12 Hormuz blockade announcement will almost certainly push retail prices higher in coming weeks. Source: AAA / BLS / EIA / AEI.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -62 pts / -0.9% from pre-war — S&P 6,816.89 (Apr 10 close, last trading session) — BEST WEEK SINCE NOV (+3.6%) NOW AT RISK — Islamabad collapse + Hormuz blockade = volatile Monday reopening
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Apr 10 close: 6,816.89 (-0.11% on day, +3.6% for week — best since November). Markets were closed Apr 11 (Sat) and Apr 12 (Sun). Nasdaq closed Apr 10 at 22,902.89 (+4.7% for week, 8-day winning streak). Dow 47,916.57 (-269 Fri; +3.0% for week). VIX 19.23 (-1.33%); Gold $4,787.40 (+2-3%, 3rd straight weekly gain); 10Y Treasury 4.31%. LMT $613.72 (-1.63% Fri, +26% YTD). RTX $201.81 (-0.68% Fri; Susquehanna Buy Apr 10). XOM ~$163.91 (+0.33% Fri, Citi PT $175). CVX $190.36 (-1.31% Fri). DAL ~$67.84 (Q1 beat Apr 8, TD Cowen PT $84). UAL ~$92 (reports Apr 22). Markets face volatile Monday reopening after Islamabad talks collapsed Sunday Apr 12 after 21 hours with no agreement, and Trump immediately declared a US naval blockade of Hormuz. Much of last week's ceasefire rally likely to reverse. March CPI 3.3% YoY, core 2.6%; UMich sentiment 47.6 all-time low. Cleveland Fed's Hammack raised prospect of rate HIKE. Fed meets Apr 28-29. Source: NYSE / Bloomberg / CNBC / BLS / UMich / Federal Reserve.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$850M/day — ISLAMABAD TALKS COLLAPSED — TRUMP DECLARES HORMUZ BLOCKADE — full force posture maintained — Bush CSG approaching Gibraltar — blockade ops will raise burn rate
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 44: ~$850M/day — Islamabad talks collapsed Apr 12 and Trump announced US Navy will 'begin the process of BLOCKADING' the Strait of Hormuz. Blockade operations, mine clearance, vessel interdictions will raise burn rate beyond ceasefire baseline. CENTCOM maintains full force posture. Historic Apr 11 transit: USS Frank E. Petersen Jr. (DDG-121) and USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112) transited the Strait — first US warships since war began — for mine-clearing. Harvard Prof. Linda Bilmes: $2B/day including all operational costs. CSIS: ~$30B total. AEI: $22.3-31B through five weeks. ~50,000+ US service members participated. Bush CSG approaching Gibraltar — ETA Red Sea Apr 14-18. Ford deployed 292 days — post-Vietnam record (294 days, Lincoln 2019-20) breaks ~Apr 14. Total battle force: 291 ships (108 deployed). Pentagon supplemental $80-100B expected (scaled down from $200B). 60-day War Powers deadline: April 29 — 17 days. Congress returns Apr 14. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / AEI / Harvard / CENTCOM / USNI Fleet Tracker.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (44 Days)
total_cost.note: ~$50.9B through Day 44 — Islamabad collapse + blockade announcement — CSIS: ~$30B — AEI: $22.3-31B — Harvard: $2B/day, $1 trillion long-term — Pentagon supplemental $80-100B expected
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12, ~$30B total. AEI: $22.3-31B for five weeks. Harvard Prof. Bilmes: $2B/day current rate, $1 trillion long-term. WarCosts.org: $35B ($236/taxpayer). Islamabad talks collapsed Apr 12; Trump's Hormuz blockade declaration signals escalation rather than drawdown. Pentagon supplemental shrunk from $200B to $80-100B (WaPo Apr 7); has NOT been formally submitted. FY2027 budget includes 387% increase for war-relevant munitions. CNN poll: 71% oppose authorizing $200B. War approval at 34% support / 53% oppose (Economist/YouGov) — lowest of any US war in modern polling. Trump Iran handling: 33% approve; overall approval 38% (record second-term low). 60-day War Powers deadline: April 29 — 17 days. Schumer announced Senate WPR vote. House Democrats called Trump 'completely unhinged' after blockade announcement. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / AEI / Harvard / WarCosts.org / WaPo / CNN / YouGov.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CEASEFIRE ON IRAN FRONT HOLDS ~120 HRS — 35,500+ cumulative — Israel 200+ Hezbollah targets over Apr 11-12 weekend — Trump declares Hormuz blockade — first US warships transited Strait Apr 11
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM Adm. Brad Cooper: 13,000+ strikes destroyed 'the military Iran built for over 40 years' — 155+ warships/submarines (largest navy elimination since WWII), 80% air defenses, 450 ballistic missile storage, 800 drone storage, 90% weapons factories, every submarine, 97% naval mines. No confirmed US/Israeli strikes on Iranian territory since 8 PM ET Apr 7 (~120 hours, ceasefire technically holds) — but Islamabad talks collapsed Apr 12 and Trump declared a US naval blockade. Historic Apr 11: USS Frank E. Petersen Jr. (DDG-121) and USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112) transited the Strait for mine-clearing — first US warships to do so since war began. Israel struck 200+ Hezbollah targets over Apr 11-12 weekend per Netanyahu. ~50,000+ US service members participated. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / Pentagon / USNI.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13 hostile KIA + 1 non-combat death — 381 WIA — no new casualties since Apr 7 ceasefire — Islamabad collapse + blockade raise risk of new casualties in coming days
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 13 killed in hostile action plus 1 non-combat death, with 381 wounded (CENTCOM Apr 8 update). Breakdown: 7 Army hostile deaths (6 in Kuwait Port Shuaiba drone strike Mar 1, 1 from Prince Sultan AB wounds), 6 Air Force non-hostile deaths (KC-135 crash in Iraq, Mar 12). The Intercept exposed discrepancies: exclusion of 200+ sailors treated after Ford fire and at least one confirmed combat-support death omitted from official rolls. Most injuries are TBI from blast overpressure. Named KIA: Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, SFC Noah Tietjens, Capt. Cody Khork. F-15E crew (494th Fighter Squadron, callsign 'Dude 44B') fully rescued Apr 4-5 from Zagros Mountains after 36-hour evasion. No new US casualties since ceasefire (8 PM ET Apr 7) — but Trump's Apr 12 Hormuz blockade declaration raises risk of IRGC engagement and direct confrontation with vessels that paid Iran's toll (Chinese, Indian, Pakistani-flagged). Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Military Times / The Intercept.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: 381 WIA (CENTCOM Apr 8) — Intercept: undercounting excludes 200+ Ford fire sailors and combat-support death — most injuries TBI — no new WIA since Apr 7 ceasefire
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. CENTCOM Apr 8 update: 381 wounded. The Intercept exposed discrepancies: exclusion of 200+ sailors treated after Ford fire (Mar 12) and at least one confirmed combat-support death omitted from official rolls. Most injuries are traumatic brain injuries from blast overpressure. Ceasefire in effect from 8 PM ET Apr 7 (~120 hours). No confirmed new WIA since ceasefire — but Islamabad collapse Apr 12 + Trump's Hormuz blockade declaration raise risk of new casualties if Iran or proxy forces engage. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Military Times / The Intercept.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: Health Min (WHO, Apr 10): 2,362 killed, 32,314 injured / HRANA: 3,540-3,636 (1,616-1,701 civilians, ~244 children) / Hengaw: 6,620 military / Iran Intl: 4,700+ security / CSM: 'at least 3,000' — no new deaths since ceasefire Apr 7
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — deeply contested across sources. Iran Health Ministry via WHO (Apr 10): 2,362 killed, 32,314 injured. HRANA (US-based) Apr 7-11: 3,540-3,636 total (1,616-1,701 civilians, ~244 children). Hengaw (Norway-based) Apr 8: 6,620 Iranian military dead alone. Iran International (Mar 31): 4,700+ security forces killed, 20,880 injured. IDF claims 6,000+ IRGC killed. Christian Science Monitor aggregate (Apr 12): 'at least 3,000' people killed in Iran. No Iranian deaths reported since ceasefire took effect Apr 7. Combined extrapolated estimate: ~9,100+. WHO: 48 hospitals and 218 health facilities damaged, 8 hospitals evacuated, 25 health workers killed, 118 injured. Displacement: 1.9-3.2M internally displaced; 68,000+ Iranians in Turkey; 35,000+ Afghans returned to Afghanistan. 4.4M Afghans in Iran particularly vulnerable. 81,000+ civilian units damaged (61,000 homes). UNHCR requires $454.2M for 2026 — only 15% funded. Source: WHO / Iran Health Ministry / HRANA / Hengaw / Iran International / UNHCR / Christian Science Monitor.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 2,020+ killed, 6,436+ wounded, 1.2M displaced (200K+ fled to Syria) — Netanyahu: 200+ Hezbollah targets over Apr 11-12 weekend — Lebanon-Israel talks Apr 15 — PM Salam postpones Washington trip
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli operations since March 2. Cumulative: 2,020+ killed, 6,436+ wounded, 1.2 million displaced (over 200,000 fled to Syria; 472 schools converted to shelters). DEADLIEST DAY: April 8 'Operation Eternal Darkness' — 50 fighter jets dropped ~160 bombs on 100 targets in 10 minutes — 254-357 killed (figures vary: Civil Defense 254, AP 303, Wikipedia 357), 1,150+ wounded. Cemetery in Shmestar struck during funeral. Continuing strikes Apr 9-12 across Beirut suburbs, southern Lebanon, Bekaa Valley. Netanyahu says Israel struck 200+ Hezbollah targets over Apr 11-12 weekend. Netanyahu: 'There is no ceasefire with Lebanon.' HISTORIC: Lebanese Ambassador Moawad and Israeli Ambassador Leiter spoke by phone Apr 11 — first official Lebanon-Israel contact since 1983. Talks set for Tuesday, Apr 15 at US State Department. Lebanese PM Nawaf Salam postponed his Washington trip citing 'current internal circumstances.' Thousands protested in Lebanon against negotiations. Leiter: 'Israel refused to discuss a ceasefire with the Hezbollah terrorist organization.' Source: Lebanon Health Ministry / IDF / UNICEF / WHO / HRW.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: 7.8M+ — Iran 1.9-3.2M IDPs — Lebanon 1.2M (200K+ fled to Syria) — 68,000+ Iranians in Turkey — 35,000+ Afghans returned to Afghanistan — UNHCR 15% funded for 2026 ($454.2M required)
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. Iran: 1.9-3.2 million internally displaced (UNHCR Mar 12 estimate — likely higher now). 68,000+ Iranians arrived in Turkey. 4.4 million Afghans in Iran particularly vulnerable; 35,000+ have returned to Afghanistan. Lebanon: 1.2 million displaced (one-sixth of population); 200,000+ fled to Syria. 472 schools converted to shelters. Regionally, conflict zones already hosted 24.3 million forcibly displaced people pre-war. UNHCR requires $454.2 million for 2026 — only 15% funded as of end-February. Strait closure crippled medical supply chains — IFRC unable to import supplies from Dubai warehouse for weeks. Ceasefire on Iran front holds but Lebanon excluded — continued Israeli operations. Source: UNHCR / WHO / OCHA / UNICEF / IFRC.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — HORMUZ SHUT — Trump declares US NAVY BLOCKADE — 600+ vessels stranded — 325 tankers — Bloomberg reports 800+ freighters — 5-10% of pre-war traffic — Apr 11 historic first US warship transit
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28. Hormuz effectively closed: traffic at 5-10% of pre-war levels (6-12 ships/day vs 100-135). Iran re-closed tanker passage after Apr 8 following Israel's 'Black Wednesday' Lebanon strikes. All 57 post-ceasefire transits used the Larak Island detour. ADNOC CEO Al Jaber (Apr 10): 'The Strait of Hormuz is not open. Access is being restricted, conditioned and controlled. That is not freedom of navigation. That is coercion.' 600+ vessels stranded (Lloyd's List, Apr 10) — 325 tankers; Bloomberg: 800+ freighters. ADNOC says 230 loaded oil tankers waiting inside Gulf. Iran's parliament passed legislation to formalize toll collection (reportedly up to $2M/vessel, accepting cryptocurrency). Countries with negotiated safe passage: Iraq, Pakistan, Japan, China, India, Malaysia, Oman. Apr 11: USS Frank E. Petersen Jr. and USS Michael Murphy transited Strait — first US warships since war began — for mine-clearing. IRGC threatened 'firm and forceful response.' Apr 12: Trump declared US Navy will blockade the Strait — complication: Iran reportedly lost track of mines it planted. Source: ADNOC / Lloyd's List / Bloomberg / IMO / IATA / CENTCOM / Trump Truth Social.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: HRANA Iran: 244+ — UNICEF Lebanon: 600 killed or injured — Iranian delegation arrived at Islamabad talks dressed in black, carrying shoes/bloodied belongings of Minab schoolchildren — Pope Leo XIV: 'listen to the voices of children'
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. HRANA: 244+ children killed in Iran (15% of casualties under 18). UNICEF reports 600 children killed or injured in Lebanon. April 8 'Operation Eternal Darkness': 254-357 killed including 33 children. Key atrocities: Minab school attack (Feb 28, ~175 killed including schoolchildren) — HRW called it a war crime; Lamerd sports hall (4 children); Karaj bridge (8 civilians). 763 schools damaged, 65+ destroyed. 30+ universities struck. At Islamabad talks Apr 11-12, the Iranian delegation arrived dressed in black — mourning Khamenei and others — carrying shoes and bloodied belongings of schoolchildren killed in the Minab strike. Pope Leo XIV at Apr 11 peace vigil: referenced 'countless letters from children in areas of conflict' and urged the world to 'listen to the voices of children.' Ceasefire on Iran front holds; Lebanon excluded — Israel 200+ Hezbollah targets over Apr 11-12 weekend. Source: UNICEF / HRANA / Lebanese MoH / WHO / HRW / Amnesty International / Vatican.
